sense.definition: a tool with a metal head and a handle, used for striking nails, etc.

examples.x: He used a hammer to drive nails into the wooden frame.
She carefully tapped the hammer to avoid splitting the wood.
He kept a hammer in his toolbox for various home repair tasks.
The hammer's weight provided the force needed for tougher jobs.

sense.definition: a versatile hand tool with a flat striking surface on one side used for driving nails into various materials, and a curved, V-shaped claw on the other side used for removing nails or prying objects apart

examples.x: He grabbed the claw hammer to drive the nails into the wooden frame.
The carpenter used the claw hammer to pull out a bent nail from the floorboard.
After finding the right size nail, she reached for the claw hammer on the workbench.
He swung the claw hammer with precision to ensure the nails went in straight.
I need a claw hammer to fix the loose door frame in the hallway.

sense.definition: a type of hammer with a rounded striking surface opposite the flat striking surface, commonly used for shaping metal, setting rivets, or striking punches in metalworking applications

examples.x: The mechanic used a ball peen hammer to shape the metal parts of the car frame.
He grabbed the ball peen hammer to smooth out the dents in the metal sheet.
The welder reached for the ball peen hammer to join the two pieces of metal together.
She used the ball peen hammer to bend the metal rod into the correct shape.
The craftsman carefully struck the metal with the ball peen hammer to ensure it was perfectly shaped.

sense.definition: a hammer-like tool with a large wooden or rubber head used for striking or directing objects

examples.x: The carpenter used a wooden mallet to gently tap the chisel into the wood.
The sculptor carefully tapped the marble block with a small mallet to shape the sculpture.
The blacksmith wielded a sturdy metal mallet to shape the red-hot iron into horseshoes.

sense.definition: a large tool consisted of a long handle with a heavy metal block at its end, used with both hands to break a stone, etc.

examples.x: He used a sledgehammer to break apart the old concrete patio.
The construction crew relied on a sledgehammer for heavy demolition work.
She found the sledgehammer effective for removing stubborn tiles.
He swung the sledgehammer with precision to avoid damaging nearby structures.
The sledgehammer's weight made it ideal for delivering powerful strikes.

sense.definition: a hammer-like tool with a rubber or soft plastic head, used for tasks that require a gentle but firm striking force, such as assembling furniture, tapping delicate surfaces, or working with materials that should not be damaged by a metal hammer

examples.x: He used a rubber mallet to gently tap the wooden pieces into place without leaving any dents.
The rubber mallet was perfect for assembling the furniture without damaging the surface.
She reached for the rubber mallet to adjust the metal frame without scratching it.
He carefully used a rubber mallet to secure the tiles without cracking them.
The mechanic used a rubber mallet to tap the parts into alignment without damaging the paint.

sense.definition: a hammer with a hollow head filled with material, designed to reduce rebound and deliver non-damaging strikes

examples.x: The mechanic used a dead blow hammer to tap the parts into place without damaging the surface.
She used a dead blow hammer to carefully fit the components together without any risk of cracking them.
He needed a dead blow hammer to adjust the metal pieces without causing any dents.
To install the tiles, they used a dead blow hammer to ensure the grout lines were even.

sense.definition: a heavy-duty hammer with a long handle, a large striking face, and a curved claw on the back, specifically designed for tasks related to framing and carpentry, such as driving large nails, framing structures, and removing nails

examples.x: The carpenter grabbed the framing hammer to drive the large nails into the wooden beams.
He swung the framing hammer with precision, making sure each nail went in straight.
She used the framing hammer to pull out old nails before replacing the damaged wood.
The heavy weight of the framing hammer helped him drive nails quickly and with less effort.
He accidentally missed the nail, but the framing hammer's large head helped him hit it with the next swing.

sense.definition: a type of hammer with a wedge-shaped peen (the end opposite the striking face), oriented perpendicular to the handle, commonly used for tasks such as shaping metal, forging, or starting and setting nails

examples.x: The blacksmith used a cross peen hammer to shape the hot metal into a horseshoe.
When working with metal, the cross peen hammer allows for more control during the shaping process.
She used the flat side of the cross peen hammer to drive the nail, and the peen to shape the metal.
The carpenter grabbed a cross peen hammer to drive the nails into the wooden frame more precisely.

sense.definition: a small hammer with a magnetized face for holding and driving small nails (tacks), commonly used in upholstery work or tasks that involve precise nail placement

examples.x: She used a tack hammer to carefully attach the fabric to the wooden frame.
The upholsterer reached for the tack hammer to fasten the new fabric onto the chair.
He kept the tack hammer nearby as he worked on his upholstery project.
With the tack hammer, he gently tapped in each small nail to secure the artwork to the wall.
She grabbed the tack hammer to put in the finishing touches on her craft project.

sense.definition: a specialized hammer with a chisel-like striking face on one side and a blunt, square face on the other side, specifically designed for tasks involving working with bricks

examples.x: He used the brick hammer to carefully split the brick into smaller pieces for the wall.
The mason picked up his brick hammer and tapped the brick into place.
She reached for the brick hammer to break the stubborn stone that was blocking the path.
After several attempts with a regular hammer, he switched to a brick hammer to get a cleaner cut.
The contractor handed me the brick hammer

sense.definition: a tool used in welding to remove slag and spatter from welded joints and clean the surface, featuring a pointed end and a chisel or flat end for chipping and cleaning purposes

examples.x: The welder used a welding hammer to remove the slag from the metal joints.
After finishing the weld, he grabbed the welding hammer to clean up the area.
She tapped the metal with a welding hammer to smooth out the edges of the weld.
He carefully held the welding hammer while striking the slag off the metal surface.

sense.definition: a small strong pointy metal that is inserted into walls or wooden objects using a hammer to hang things from or fasten them together

examples.x: He used a nail to secure the wooden boards in place.
The box of nails included various sizes for different projects.
She carefully hammered the nail to avoid splitting the wood.
He removed the old nail from the wall to hang a new picture.
She checked that each nail was driven in straight for a neat finish.

sense.definition: a strong and large nail with a flat, broad head, commonly used in framing and construction projects

examples.x: The carpenter used a framing nail to secure the wooden beams in place.
She picked up a box of framing nails before starting the construction project.
The contractor used a nail gun to quickly drive the framing nails into the studs.
He carefully hammered in each framing nail to ensure the wall would hold up.
The builder recommended using framing nails instead of regular nails for the foundation.

sense.definition: a small nail with a discreet head, commonly used for finishing and trim work in woodworking projects

examples.x: The carpenter used a finish nail to attach the trim around the door without leaving noticeable holes.
To secure the baseboard, she hammered in several finish nails along the edges.
He preferred using finish nails for the molding, as they would not leave large indentations in the wood.
After installing the cabinets, the contractor added finish nails to hold the decorative panels in place.
She carefully placed each finish nail to ensure the corners of the frame aligned perfectly.

sense.definition: a thin, small-gauge nail designed for delicate woodworking tasks such as attaching trim, moldings, or other lightweight materials

examples.x: The carpenter used a brad nail to attach the decorative trim to the cabinet.
She carefully inserted brad nails into the wooden frame to avoid splitting the edges.
He chose brad nails for the project because they leave barely noticeable holes.
The picture frame was assembled using a few well-placed brad nails for a clean finish.
When working with thin wood pieces, brad nails help keep the material intact and stable.

sense.definition: a large nail with a wide, flat head and a rubber or neoprene washer, used for securing roofing materials to the roof surface

examples.x: Make sure to use rust-resistant roofing nails for outdoor projects.
The box of roofing nails was enough to finish the entire roof installation.
He chose roofing nails with coated steel to prevent corrosion over time.
The worker checked that all the roofing nails were driven flush with the surface.

sense.definition: a type of nail specifically designed for fastening materials to concrete or masonry surfaces

examples.x: The carpenter used a concrete nail to attach the wooden frame securely to the brick wall.
He hammered a concrete nail into the wall to hang the heavy shelving unit.
The builder used hardened steel concrete nails to secure the formwork to the foundation.
She bought a pack of concrete nails for the renovation project to mount the wooden panels on the concrete wall.

sense.definition: a hardened steel nail designed for fastening materials to masonry surfaces like concrete or brick

examples.x: The carpenter used a masonry nail to attach the wooden beam to the brick wall.
He hammered the masonry nail into the stone foundation with a heavy-duty hammer.
The builder recommended using masonry nails for the outdoor project to ensure durability.
She selected masonry nails to mount the metal brackets onto the stone structure.
Always wear safety goggles when driving a masonry nail into hard surfaces.

sense.definition: a nail with a textured, ring-like pattern along the shank, offering increased holding power and resistance to withdrawal

examples.x: The carpenter used a ring shank nail to secure the deck boards, ensuring they would stay in place for years.
To prevent the nails from loosening, he chose ring shank nails for the fence posts.
She decided to use ring shank nails to fasten the wooden beams, as they are less likely to pull out over time.
The deck construction went smoothly with the help of ring shank nails, which held the planks tightly together.
The contractor relied on ring shank nails to attach the roofing felt, knowing they would stay secure in the harsh weather.

sense.definition: a nail with two heads, designed for temporary or removable fastening purposes

examples.x: The contractor used duplex nails to secure the temporary scaffolding until the permanent structure was in place.
To assemble the wooden frame, he hammered in several duplex nails for easy removal later.
The workers appreciated the duplex nails because they made dismantling the temporary setup much quicker.
For the temporary formwork, the builder chose duplex nails, knowing they would be easy to take out when the job was done.
During the renovation, the construction crew relied on duplex nails to fasten the drywall, as they could be pulled out when needed.

sense.definition: a large-headed nail used for securing roofing materials to wood surfaces

examples.x: The roofer used clout nails to secure the roofing felt to the wooden beams.
For the garden shed, we used clout nails to attach the thin metal sheets to the frame.
When fixing the tarpaper, clout nails were the best option for a secure hold without visible holes.
The carpenter chose clout nails for the lightweight insulation to avoid damaging the material.
For this project, I need clout nails to fasten the thin plastic sheeting without leaving big marks.

sense.definition: a type of nail specifically designed for securing panels, such as plywood or particle board, to wooden frames or studs

examples.x: The carpenter used panel nails to attach the plywood to the frame of the cabinet.
For the new fence, he chose panel nails to ensure the wood panels stayed securely in place.
She carefully hammered the panel nails into the thin sheet metal to avoid bending it.
The builder recommended using panel nails for attaching the panels to the wall in the living room.

sense.definition: a small nail with a decorative head used for attaching upholstery fabric to furniture frames

examples.x: She carefully hammered in the upholstery nails to secure the fabric to the wooden frame.
The antique chair was restored with brass upholstery nails to give it an elegant look.
The craftsman added decorative upholstery nails along the edges to enhance the sofa's design.
He used a hammer to gently push the upholstery nails into place, ensuring the leather stayed tight.
The designer recommended adding upholstery nails around the back of the bench to give it a polished finish.

sense.definition: a type of nail made by cutting or shaping a solid piece of steel, often used in carpentry and woodworking

examples.x: The carpenter used a cut nail to secure the hardwood flooring in place.
The old house had original cut nails, a sign of its age and craftsmanship.
He carefully hammered each cut nail into the wooden beam to ensure it held tightly.
The restoration team replaced the damaged floorboards with new ones, securing them with cut nails.
To maintain the authentic look of the vintage door, they used cut nails instead of modern screws.

sense.definition: a small nail with a flat, wide head, used to temporarily attach lightweight materials to a surface
a nail with helical threads along the shank, offering increased holding power and stability when driven into materials

examples.x: The carpenter used screw-shank nails to secure the wooden planks to the floor, ensuring they stayed in place.
For the heavy framing work, the contractor recommended using screw-shank nails to prevent the wood from shifting.
To fasten the plywood sheets, they opted for screw-shank nails to ensure the joints would stay tight over time.
When attaching the beams to the posts, the builders used screw-shank nails for added strength and stability.
The contractor explained that screw-shank nails would provide a better grip than regular nails, especially in the softwood material.

sense.definition: a sturdy metal tool with a pointed end used to drive or remove pins, bolts, or other fasteners

examples.x: The mechanic used a drift punch to align the holes before inserting the pin.
The metalworker employed a drift punch to adjust the metal parts without causing any damage.
He used the drift punch to gently tap the piece into alignment with the other components.
After drilling, he used a drift punch to make sure the hole was clear and ready for the next step.

sense.definition: a type of nail specifically designed for attaching siding materials, such as vinyl, wood, or fiber cement, to exterior walls or other surfaces

examples.x: The contractor used siding nails to attach the wooden panels to the house's exterior.
Make sure to choose the right length of siding nails to securely fasten the material.
He ran out of siding nails and had to make a quick trip to the hardware store.
When installing the new siding, it's important to space the siding nails evenly for a uniform look.
The house was completely re-sided, with each panel held in place by stainless steel siding nails.

sense.definition: a specialized nail with ridges along its shank, used for joining wood pieces together

examples.x: The carpenter used corrugated nails to secure the plywood sheets to the frame of the shed.
For added strength, she opted for corrugated nails to fasten the panels together during the furniture repair.
The DIY enthusiast preferred corrugated nails for her wood project, as they provided a firmer hold than regular nails.
Corrugated nails are ideal for joining thin wooden boards without risking splintering or shifting.
When installing the subfloor, the builder chose corrugated nails to ensure that the panels stayed in place.

sense.definition: a small, slender pin used for attaching lightweight materials to surfaces

examples.x: The locksmith carefully used an escutcheon pin to secure the decorative plate on the door lock.
She added a few escutcheon pins to the cabinet to hold the ornamental metal plates in place.
The antique door featured escutcheon pins that were barely noticeable but essential for holding the hardware in position.
The brass escutcheon pins matched the finish of the door handle, enhancing the overall aesthetic.
When restoring the old chest, he used escutcheon pins to reattach the decorative metal plate.

sense.definition: a specialized nail designed for securing carpets or rugs to floors

examples.x: The installer used carpet nails to keep the edges of the rug securely in place.
When laying down new carpeting, make sure to use carpet nails for a tight fit.
The professional installer used carpet nails to ensure the carpet stayed firmly attached to the wooden floor.
She carefully placed the carpet nails to prevent any wrinkles from forming in the carpet.

sense.definition: a type of nail specifically designed for fastening flooring materials, such as hardwood, engineered wood, or laminate, to subflooring or underlayment

examples.x: The contractor used flooring nails to secure the hardwood planks to the subfloor.
When installing the new floors, make sure to use flooring nails to avoid any movement.
He carefully hammered each flooring nail into the wood to prevent any gaps between the planks.
The box of flooring nails was specifically chosen for the thickness of the floorboards.
After applying the adhesive, they added flooring nails for extra stability.

sense.definition: a machine or tool used for making holes in paper or other material

examples.x: She used a three-hole punch to organize her documents into a binder.
The office manager bought a new punch for the department's paperwork needs.
He used a single-hole punch to make holes in the corners of his flashcards.
The craft store offered decorative punches for creating intricate designs in paper.
The punch on his desk had a lever for easy operation when punching through thick stacks of paper.

sense.definition: a striking tool used to create small, controlled indentations or marks on a workpiece to serve as reference points for drilling or other operations
a striking tool used to forcefully remove pins, rivets, or similar fasteners from a workpiece by striking it with a hammer or mallet

examples.x: He used a pin punch to carefully remove the old dowel pin from the machinery.
To disassemble the engine, she needed a pin punch to take out the cotter pins.
The mechanic grabbed the pin punch and tapped it with a hammer to loosen the stubborn pin.
After hours of trying, the only way to get the pin out was with a pin punch.
I always keep a pin punch in my toolbox for quick pin removals during repairs.

sense.definition: a precision hand tool with a sharp, pointed tip that is used to create small, accurate indentations or pilot marks on a workpiece

examples.x: The mechanic used a prick punch to mark the exact spot for drilling the hole.
Before drilling into the metal, he made a small dent with the prick punch to guide the drill bit.
She carefully tapped the prick punch onto the wood to create a mark for the screw placement.
The technician used a prick punch to ensure the hole would be drilled in the right spot on the engine part.
The carpenter marked the surface with a prick punch before starting the work on the wooden frame.
